question.text: Payment banks licensed by RBI can perform all of the following EXCEPT:

answer.text: Accept savings deposits up to Rs. 2,00,000

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Payment banks cannot undertake any lending activities. They can accept deposits (up to Rs. 2,00,000), issue ATM/debit cards (not credit cards), and provide payment/remittance services. They promote financial inclusion for low-income households. **The Trap:** The deposit limit was originally Rs. 1,00,000 and has been revised. Payment banks can accept deposits but cannot lend — this is their defining restriction. **Cross-Exam Context:** Banking system structure is tested in X-A (Ch 5) and is relevant to VII (Securities Operations). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Payment banks are great for transactions but not for lending — clients needing loans must approach commercial banks or NBFCs.
